coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: You're 9 weeks out and in taper—this is your Saturday long run, but volume is now the priority over intensity. Target 90–110 minutes at Z2 (130–145 bpm based on yesterday's 140 avg), focusing on the downhill and technical footwork you'll need in the Julian Alps. Yesterday's 10.21 mi @ 1,155 ft suggests you can handle the vert load; today, dial back to ~1,000–1,200 ft of climbing and nail the descents. Your recovery score is solid at 81/100 and sleep last night was exceptional (9.62h)—you're ready, but don't chase pace. Taper is about feeling fresh and confident on race day, not hitting PRs now.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: With a 90–110 min effort in Z2, you'll burn roughly 1,200–1,400 kcal; take 30–40g carbs per hour on the move (sports drink, gels, or real food if you're running locally). Post-run, eat within 30 minutes: 40g carb + 15–20g protein (rice + chicken, pasta + egg, or a recovery drink) to replenish glycogen before your next meal. You've logged solid long runs all cycle—don't experiment with nutrition today. Stick to what's worked. Hydration: 500–750 ml per hour depending on heat and terrain.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Last night's 9h 38m sleep at 91% efficiency is elite-level recovery; your 63 bpm resting HR (vs. 30-day avg of 61.6) is still low and healthy. Maintain that sleep standard through taper—aim for 8–9h nightly, in a cool dark room, with no screens after 10 PM. After today's run, foam roll your calves and quads for 8–10 minutes, then easy walking or swimming if you feel stiff. You're carrying low fatigue (19.2 mi this week vs. 57.1 last week), so active recovery beats complete rest. Sunday should be a shakeout run (20–30 min Z2) or rest day—no hard work.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 69 days from Slovenia. Taper can mess with your head—you'll feel rested and want to test yourself. Don't. Every run from now is about arrival, not arrival with a W. Use today's long run to visualize the race: the climb out of Kranjska Gora, the exposed ridge section, how you'll pace the final 10K when your quads are screaming. You've built the fitness; the next 9 weeks are about protecting it and showing up sharp. Trust the cycle—you've executed at a high level all season.
